results.prompt: possible conjugates for progressive supranuclear palsy

metrics.responseText:
based on the prompt alone, i’m provisionally reading it this way: progressive supranuclear palsy looks most like a neurodegeneration / pathway modulation / barrier-limited case. the current mechanism read leans toward pathway modulation. the main delivery constraints right now are blood-brain barrier access. what the planner could confidently pull out so far: recognized disease: progressive supranuclear palsy; biology read: neurodegeneration; therapeutic read: pathway modulation; delivery read: barrier-limited. grounded themes: chronic non-oncology, cns / bbb, neurodegeneration. the most plausible strategy lanes right now are bbb-shuttled oligo / gene-modulation delivery, neuroprotective / pathway-modulating or proteostasis-support biologic logic, mitochondrial / proteostasis support conjugates, neuron- or glia-targeted delivery as a high-risk conditional lane, classical cytotoxic conjugates are a weak-fit comparator. ranking is still withheld because the target, entry handle, and construct logic are not yet specific enough.
confidence
insufficient

biology is still too underdefined for a fully confident recommendation. the system is intentionally abstaining from a responsible winner and treating any ranking below as provisional only.
